attachment.snippet:
1	# viewrr — Context Glossary
2	
3	Ubiquitous language for the viewrr P2P SVOD platform. Definitions only — no
4	implementation. When a term here conflicts with usage in a design doc, this file wins
5	(or the conflict gets resolved and this file updated).
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Identity
10	
11	The user's permanent cryptographic identity: an Ed25519 keypair. The `publicKey`
12	**is** the account (the "viewrr ID"). Derived solely from the **Recovery Phrase** —
13	never from the Master Password. The server never holds any part of the secret.
14	
15	## Recovery Phrase
16	
17	A BIP39 mnemonic (12 words default, 24 optional) that is the **sole root of trust**
18	for an Identity. `mnemonic → seed → DHT.keyPair` is the one and only identity
19	derivation. Entering the phrase on any device reproduces the exact same Identity.
20	Never transmitted, stored, or logged.
21	
22	*Resolved Q1: the mnemonic — not the Master Password — is the identity root. The
23	earlier `Argon2id(masterPassword)→keypair` derivation is retired; it would have made
24	recovery reconstruct a different keypair.*
25	
26	## Master Password
27	
28	A **local unlock secret only**. Encrypts the at-rest `secretKey` (and vaults) on a
29	single device. Changing it re-encrypts the local blob and does **not** change the
30	Identity. Not synced, not the identity seed.
31	
32	## Content Key
33	
34	A per-title 32-byte AES key. Sealed to the user's `publicKey` on entitlement, opened
35	with their `secretKey` inside the Bare worklet, then used to derive per-segment
36	AES-128 keys + IVs via HKDF. This is **self-custody clear-key** protection — decrypt
37	happens in app memory. There is no hardware DRM and no license server (see
38	`docs/adr/p2p-0001`). Anti-capture is limited to OS window flags (forensic watermark
39	deferred out of MVP), not cryptographic prevention.
40	
41	## Entitlement
42	
43	The **permanent** right to a title, embodied by a self-custody Content Key sealed to
44	the user's `publicKey`. Once acquired, a title is **owned forever** — the server
45	cannot revoke it (it holds no `secretKey`). Playback is offline and client-side. See
46	`docs/adr/p2p-0004`.
47	
48	## Subscription
49	
50	A gate on **what a user may newly acquire/download**, plus seeding perks and storage
51	tier. A Subscription does **not** revoke already-owned Entitlements. viewrr is an
52	ownership model, not enforceable rental — despite legacy "SVOD" wording in older docs.
53	(Subscriptions/payments are deferred out of MVP — see `docs/adr/p2p-0005`.)
54	
55	## NAS
56	
57	Your homelab node. Its viewrr role is **DHT bootstrap + Ktor metadata registry** only
58	(plus an optional *paid* backup tier later). It is **not** a content origin/seeder —
59	content originates from users' own devices. See `docs/adr/p2p-0005`. (Legacy docs calling
60	the NAS "origin seeder of all content variants" are superseded.)
61	
62	## Catalog
63	
64	The **central, searchable index** of titles available in the mesh, powered by ParadeDB
65	/ pg_search. Media files are decentralized (no central media server); the **catalog is
66	centralized**. Any peer acquiring a title contributes its metadata + availability, so
67	the catalog grows from mesh activity. Titles are keyed by **content UUID** (TMDB ID →
68	UUID v5) so the same film from different uploaders dedups to one entry. viewrr *is* the
69	index — this is the product's core SVOD differentiator, and its main legal exposure
70	(needs a takedown pipeline). See `docs/adr/p2p-0008` (supersedes the earlier "no catalog"
71	stance in `P2P-ADR 0005`).
72	
73	## Availability
74	
75	The fact that some peer holds a given title. Discovered **P2P via the DHT**
76	(`hash(contentUUID)` swarm), never from a central `publicKey ↔ title` table. All copies
77	of the same format are interchangeable sources — no copy is canonical. See `docs/adr/p2p-0008`.
78	
79	## Peer Selection
80	
81	Choosing *which* available peer to pull from: **nearest by Plus Code**, then **fastest
82	uplink**, with a fallback chain to the next-best peer. Entirely client-side. Governs
83	*where to pull*, not *what is authentic*. See `docs/adr/p2p-0009`.
84	
85	## Storage Pool
86	
87	The union of storage slices contributed by a user's devices — each device dedicates
88	**≥20% of free space**. The pool hosts and seeds that user's private vault and their
89	publicly-seeded content. There is no central media server; a user's own device pool is
90	the origin for their content. See `docs/adr/p2p-0011`.
91	
92	## Backup Tier
93	
94	An optional durability service: the NAS stores a user's **ciphertext-only** private
95	originals (no key, no plaintext, no backdoor). It is the single-device durability escape
96	hatch against total data loss. Ships functionally in MVP on jobin-nas; billing deferred
97	to phase-2. See `docs/adr/p2p-0011`.
98	
99	## De-index
100	
101	The operator's **only** moderation power: removing a title's row from the central
102	Catalog so it is no longer searchable. It does **not** delete the file — bytes remain
103	reachable via direct public link. No backdoor, no key escrow, no file deletion. See
104	`docs/adr/p2p-0010`.
105	
106	## Channel (phase 2)
107	
108	A creator-owned publishing space (SoundCloud/Dailymotion-style) for the creator's
109	**own rights-cleared media**. Channels are the paid layer, deferred to phase 2 — a
110	distinct opt-in publishing model layered on top of the neutral infrastructure base.
111	Not part of MVP.

1	# P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture (Proposal Epic)
2	
3	> **Status: proposal / migration epic — not shipped.** This documents a deliberate
4	> re-architecture of viewrr away from the shipped Hub/Node + Keycloak + central-serving
5	> design toward a Pear/Holepunch P2P + mnemonic self-custody design.
6	>
7	> ⚠️ **Numbering caution:** the ADRs referenced here (P2P-ADR 0001–0012) are an
8	> **independent set** living in `files(1)/docs/adr/p2p-*.md` — they are **not** the shipped
9	> [ADR Index](ADR-Index) (`docs/adr/0001–0004`), despite overlapping numbers. Read the
10	> title, not the number.
11	>
12	> ⚠️ **ponytail note:** ~7 of these replace or duplicate systems that already ship
13	> (Hub/Node serving, Keycloak auth, egress-IP locality, central Title/Copy availability).
14	> This is a two-architecture migration, not additive work. Cost is real; see per-issue
15	> warnings.
16	
...

36	
37	0004 (ownership), 0010 (de-index moderation), 0012 (no fingerprinting), and "no Widevine
38	license server" from 0001 — these cost little and don't require the P2P rewrite.
39

- Milestone **#20** (epic) · 11 issues **#120–130** · wiki proposal page + sidebar link
- 12 `P2P-ADR`s + `CONTEXT.md` glossary in `files (1)/`
- 16 design forks grilled; ponytail flagged ~7 as rebuild/duplicate vs shipped — recorded on every issue.

One thing still true and worth a beat: the epic is a **real two-architecture migration** off the auth system (#112–115) actively being built. Issues are framed as such, but if this proceeds, those Keycloak issues need explicit close/redirect. Say the word and I'll link/close them against the epic.

content.text: Closing #112, #114, #115 (Keycloak setup/migration) as superseded by the epic. #104 is web-client work that only *uses* Keycloak auth — I'll retarget it with a comment, not close it. #113 already closed.

input.command:
REPO="viewrr/viewrr"
NOTE=$'Superseded by the **P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture** epic (milestone #20). Identity moves from Keycloak/OIDC to a mnemonic-derived Ed25519 self-custody keypair — see #120 (P2P-ADR 0001). Closing as not planned under the current direction; reopen if the epic is dropped.'
for n in 112 114 115; do
  gh issue comment "$n" --repo "$REPO" --body "$NOTE" >/dev/null
  gh issue close "$n" --repo "$REPO" --reason "not planned" >/dev/null && echo "closed #$n"
done
# #104: retarget, keep open
gh issue comment 104 --repo "$REPO" --body $'Note: the **Keycloak auth** portion here is superseded by the P2P self-custody identity epic (#120, milestone #20) — the web client would obtain a short-lived token scoped to `publicKey` rather than Keycloak/OIDC. The "viewrr API client" work remains valid. Retargeting auth mechanism; leaving open.' >/dev/null && echo "commented #104 (kept open)"
echo "--- open auth issues now ---"
gh issue list --repo "$REPO" --state open --label area:auth --json number,title --jq '.[]|"#\(.number) \(.title)"'

Done. All three Keycloak issues closed as **not planned**, each commented pointing to epic #20 / #120:

- **#112** Keycloak realm + OIDC client — closed
- **#114** Google OAuth + passkeys/WebAuthn — closed
- **#115** Retire /auth/login + argon2 → Keycloak — closed

- #113 (OIDC resource server) was already closed.

Auth direction on the repo now points to self-custody (#120), Keycloak work stood down. Reversible — reopen if the epic gets dropped.

Session complete: epic #20 + issues #120–130, wiki proposal page, 12 `P2P-ADR`s + glossary in `files (1)/`, collision cleaned, Keycloak stood down.

input.command:
SRC="/Users/jobinlawrance/Project/tiny/files (1)"
cd REDACTED.main
git checkout -q -b docs/p2p-proposal
cp "$SRC"/docs/adr/p2p-*.md docs/adr/
cp "$SRC"/CONTEXT.md docs/adr/CONTEXT-p2p.md
cat > docs/adr/p2p-INDEX.md <<'EOF'
# P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture — ADR set (proposal)

> **Not shipped.** Design record for the P2P/self-custody migration epic
> (milestone #20). Independent of the shipped ADRs `0001–0005` in this directory,
> despite overlapping numbers — these are prefixed `p2p-`. See the wiki page
> **P2P Re-architecture** and issues #120–#130.

| P2P-ADR | Decision | Issue |
|--------|----------|-------|
| [p2p-0001](p2p-0001-self-custody-clearkey-no-hardware-drm.md) | Self-custody clear-key, no DRM/license server | #122 |
| [p2p-0002](p2p-0002-compose-desktop-libvlc-drop-electron.md) | Compose Desktop + player; drop Electron | #130 |
| [p2p-0003](p2p-0003-bare-worklet-embedding-per-platform.md) | Bare worklet embedding per platform | #121 |
| [p2p-0004](p2p-0004-ownership-model-not-rental.md) | Ownership model, not rental | #123 |
| [p2p-0005](p2p-0005-neutral-infrastructure-user-hosted.md) | Neutral infra (superseded by p2p-0008) | — |
| [p2p-0006](p2p-0006-private-discovery-topics-and-pairing.md) | Secret-derived topics + pairing | #126 |
| [p2p-0007](p2p-0007-single-crypto-stack-libsodium-in-worklet.md) | Single crypto stack (libsodium/worklet) | #130 |
| [p2p-0008](p2p-0008-central-catalog-mesh-contributed.md) | Central catalog, pseudonymous DHT availability | #124 |
| [p2p-0009](p2p-0009-peer-selection-proximity-uplink.md) | Peer selection: Plus Code + uplink | #125 |
| [p2p-0010](p2p-0010-operator-power-deindex-only-no-backdoor.md) | De-index-only, no backdoor | #128 |
| [p2p-0011](p2p-0011-multi-device-storage-pool.md) | Storage pool + encrypted backup | #127 |
| [p2p-0012](p2p-0012-no-device-fingerprinting.md) | No device fingerprinting | #129 |

Glossary: [CONTEXT-p2p.md](CONTEXT-p2p.md)
EOF
git add -A
git -c user.email="jobinlawrance@gmail.com" -c user.name="jobinlawrance" commit -q -m "docs: add P2P/self-custody proposal ADR set (epic #20)"
git push -q -u origin docs/p2p-proposal 2>&1 | tail -3

- **Wiki** repointed off the orphaned `files(1)/` path → in-repo `docs/adr/p2p-*.md` (PR #131). No dangling links.

**Full epic state on `viewrr/viewrr`:**
- Milestone **#20** · issues **#120–#130** (all `P2P-ADR`-referenced) · Keycloak **#112/#114/#115** closed not-planned · **#104** retargeted
- Wiki **P2P Re-architecture** proposal page + sidebar
- **PR #131** carrying the 12 ADRs + glossary into `docs/adr/`

All reversible: PR unmerged, closed issues reopenable. Merge #131 to make it official, or close it + reopen the Keycloak issues to abandon the pivot.

Nothing left dangling. Next real step is yours: **merge #131** (commit the direction) or start executing an issue — #120 (identity) is the root dependency; everything else hangs off it.

content.text:
Stop — firing 11 agents to "close" these in parallel would produce garbage and false progress. Four hard blockers, then the version that actually works:

1. **Wrong repo.** We're in `tiny` (TinyRaven). The issues are `viewrr/viewrr`. Worktrees are repo-scoped — they'd branch tiny, not viewrr. I have a viewrr clone in scratchpad; worktrees must come off that.

2. **Not parallelizable — it's a dependency chain, not a fan-out.** #120 (mnemonic identity) and #121 (Bare/Holepunch P2P core) are the *foundations*. #124 (DHT catalog), #125 (peer selection), #126 (topics), #127 (pool) all require the DHT/worklet from #121 to even exist. Spawn them in parallel and 7 agents build on a transport layer that isn't there. You can't fan out a rewrite whose base layers aren't built.

3. **Not one-session "closeable."** Each is a multi-week architectural rewrite (rip out Keycloak; build a P2P core from scratch). "Close" = ship working code. Agents would emit stubs and *mark architectural epics done* — the exact confident-wrong trap ponytail exists to prevent. Dishonest progress.

4. **Contradicts the verdict we just recorded.** Ponytail flagged ~7 of these as rebuilding/duplicating shipping systems. Building all 11 = doing the flagged over-engineering, at fleet scale.

**What parallel agents + worktrees + caveman/ponytail CAN actually do well — pick one:**

- **A — Implement + close the additive subset (recommended).** The 3-4 genuinely bounded, dependency-free ones: **#123** ownership policy (docs/semantics), **#128** de-index (small blocklist table + TMDB gate), **#129** no-fingerprint (guard/doc), plus the removal half of **#122** (drop license-server path). These don't need the P2P core, parallelize cleanly, and can be *honestly finished + closed*. Real worktrees, real PRs, real closes.

- **B — Scaffold the big rewrites as draft PRs (not closed).** One agent per foundational issue (#120, #121) produces an implementation plan + skeleton in its own worktree → draft PR for your review. Sequential-aware, honestly labeled "spike," never auto-closed.

I'll spawn the team the moment you pick. Default rec: **A** — it's the only set where "close them in parallel" is true instead of theater. Which?

lastPrompt: create worktress and use /caveman:caveman and /ponytail:ponytail and work on these issues and close them in parallel using agent teams

**Parallel set = 3 genuinely bounded, dependency-free issues:** #123 (ownership policy), #128 (de-index blocklist + TMDB gate — the one with real code against the shipped central catalog), #129 (no-fingerprint decision). Setting up worktrees off the viewrr clone.

content.text:
On Bouncy Castle: **not in this batch** — none of #123/#128/#129 touch crypto (ownership policy, a DB blocklist + TMDB HTTP call, a decision doc). And the standing rule (P2P-ADR 0007) holds: **no Bouncy Castle anywhere** — crypto lives in libsodium/worklet, never a second JVM stack. Your instinct is right though, and I'm baking it into the agents: **reuse the app's existing deps** (Ktor HTTP client, Exposed ORM, Kotlin stdlib) — zero new libraries, don't reinvent.

Spawning 3 Engineer agents in parallel, one per worktree, caveman+ponytail ethos, explore-then-minimal-change, open PRs (I close the issues after review — not the agents).

input.description: Issue #123 ownership not rental
input.prompt:
You are working in a git worktree of the `viewrr/viewrr` repo (Kotlin/Ktor self-hosted media server, a Jellyfin-style OTT platform with a central Hub + Nodes and a central catalog). Your ONLY working directory:

REDACTED

cd there first. Branch is already `p2p/123-ownership` off origin/main.

TASK — GitHub issue viewrr/viewrr#123 "[P2P] Ownership model, not enforceable rental":
The decision (P2P-ADR 0004): acquiring a title = permanent ownership; a subscription gates NEW acquisition/perks/storage tier ONLY and must NEVER revoke already-owned titles. Retention/inactivity cleanup is separate from entitlement revocation.

ETHOS (mandatory):
- CAVEMAN: terse output. Fragments fine. Code/commits written normally.
- PONYTAIL (lazy senior dev): smallest honest change. Reuse existing code/deps — Ktor HTTP client, Exposed ORM, Kotlin stdlib. ZERO new dependencies. NO Bouncy Castle. Do NOT invent abstractions. YAGNI.
- HONESTY: if there is no revocation/subscription-expiry code to change, the property is ALREADY satisfied — say so and record it as a short docs note, do NOT manufacture code to look busy.

STEPS:
1. Explore the repo (grep for subscription, entitlement, revoke, expire, access, license). Determine: does any code revoke or gate playback of already-acquired content on subscription lapse?
2. If such code exists: make the minimal change so owned titles are never revoked; add a `ponytail:` comment explaining.
3. If it does NOT exist (likely): add a concise `docs/adr/p2p-0004-status.md` (or append to existing docs) stating the ownership invariant is satisfied — no revocation-on-lapse logic exists — so future changes preserve it. One small self-check only if you wrote real logic.
4. Commit (clear message referencing #123), push branch `p2p/123-ownership`, open a PR against `main` with `gh pr create`, body referencing #123 and milestone "P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture". Do NOT close the issue.

RETURN (concise): what you found, what you changed (or "doc-only, already satisfied"), the PR URL, and an honest completion status (done / needs-human). gh is authenticated. Do not touch other worktrees or files outside your dir.

input.description: Issue #128 de-index + TMDB gate
input.prompt:
You are working in a git worktree of the `viewrr/viewrr` repo (Kotlin/Ktor self-hosted media server; central Hub + Nodes; central catalog with a Title/Copy model and tmdbId matching). Your ONLY working directory:

REDACTED

cd there first. Branch is already `p2p/128-deindex` off origin/main.

TASK — GitHub issue viewrr/viewrr#128 "[P2P] De-index-only moderation + TMDB allowlist":
Two concrete, additive capabilities against the EXISTING central catalog:
1. **De-index blocklist:** an operator can mark a Title/tmdbId as de-indexed so it is excluded from browse/search results (the row/file is NOT deleted — just hidden from the catalog).
2. **TMDB allowlist gate:** a Title with no tmdbId match is treated as private-by-default — never surfaced in the public catalog/browse/search.

ETHOS (mandatory):
- CAVEMAN: terse output. Code/commits normal.
- PONYTAIL (lazy senior): smallest honest change. Reuse the EXISTING schema (Exposed), existing catalog/browse/search queries, existing TMDB client if present. ZERO new dependencies. NO Bouncy Castle. Prefer a single boolean column + a query filter over a new subsystem. YAGNI.
- HONESTY: implement only what the shipped code actually supports. If the catalog/browse code isn't present in this repo state, document precisely what you found and what the minimal change WOULD be, and mark needs-human — do not fabricate.

STEPS:
1. Explore: find the Title/Copy models, the browse/search endpoints, the DB layer (Exposed migrations), and any TMDB matching code (issue #82 shipped "Title/Copy split + tmdbId matching").
2. Implement the smallest real version: e.g. a `deindexed BOOLEAN DEFAULT false` (or a small blocklist table) + filter it (and `tmdbId IS NULL`) out of public browse/search queries. Add a migration consistent with the repo's existing migration approach. Add ONE small test/self-check on the filter (assert de-indexed + non-tmdb rows are excluded).
3. Add a `ponytail:` comment marking the deliberate simplicity + upgrade path.
4. Commit (message referencing #128), push branch `p2p/128-deindex`, open a PR against `main` referencing #128 + milestone "P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture". Do NOT close the issue.

RETURN (concise): files touched, the migration + filter you added, the PR URL, test result, honest completion status. gh is authenticated. Stay inside your worktree dir only.

input.description: Issue #129 no fingerprinting
input.prompt:
You are working in a git worktree of the `viewrr/viewrr` repo (Kotlin/Ktor media server). Your ONLY working directory:

REDACTED

cd there first. Branch is already `p2p/129-no-fingerprint` off origin/main.

TASK — GitHub issue viewrr/viewrr#129 "[P2P] No device/browser fingerprinting (rejected)":
This is a DECISION issue — a "won't build." Device/browser fingerprinting for Sybil resistance is rejected (contradicts permissionless identity, privacy-hostile, only touches web tier, evadable). Catalogue integrity is handled at the data layer instead (TMDB allowlist + content checks).

ETHOS (mandatory):
- CAVEMAN: terse. Code/commits normal.
- PONYTAIL (lazy senior, ULTRA here): this is a deletion/decision, not a feature. The laziest correct outcome is a small durable record of the decision, NOT new code. Do NOT build anything. YAGNI to the max.
- HONESTY: if there is fingerprinting code already present in the repo, that changes things — search for it (grep: fingerprint, canvas, deviceId, user-agent hashing). If found, note it (removal candidate) and mark needs-human. If NOT present (expected), there is nothing to remove.

STEPS:
1. Grep the repo to confirm no fingerprinting code exists.
2. Write a concise decision record: `docs/adr/p2p-0012-status.md` (or append to a security/decisions doc if one exists) — one short page: fingerprinting rejected, why, and the data-layer alternative (TMDB allowlist per #128). Reference issue #129 + P2P-ADR 0012.
3. Commit (message referencing #129), push branch `p2p/129-no-fingerprint`, open a PR against `main` referencing #129 + milestone "P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture". Do NOT close the issue.

RETURN (concise): grep result (fingerprinting present? y/n), the doc you wrote, PR URL, honest completion status (this one should be "done, doc-only"). gh is authenticated. Stay in your worktree dir only.

content.content:
diff --git a/docs/adr/p2p-0012-status.md b/docs/adr/p2p-0012-status.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..5fc352d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/adr/p2p-0012-status.md
@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+---
+status: rejected
+issue: viewrr/viewrr#129
+p2p-adr: 0012
+milestone: P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture
+---
+
+# No device/browser fingerprinting for Sybil resistance (rejected)
+
+Device/browser fingerprinting (canvas hashing, user-agent hashing, `navigator`
+entropy, `deviceId` derivation) as a Sybil-resistance mechanism is **rejected**.
+This is a decision record, not a feature — nothing is built.
+
+## Why rejected
+
+- **Contradicts permissionless identity.** The P2P self-custody re-architecture is
+  built on identity that anyone can hold without gatekeeping. Fingerprinting is a
+  covert per-device gate — the opposite of permissionless.
+- **Privacy-hostile.** Silent client fingerprinting tracks users without consent and
+  is exactly the surveillance pattern a self-custody media server exists to avoid.
+- **Only touches the web tier.** Fingerprinting is a browser-side signal; native
+  clients (AFinity/CMP mobile, TV) never produce it. A defense that only covers one
+  of three surfaces is not a defense.
+- **Evadable.** Fingerprints are trivially spoofed/rotated (headless browsers,
+  UA overrides, VMs). It raises effort for honest users far more than for a Sybil
+  attacker.
+
+## Alternative (where integrity actually lives)
+
+Catalogue integrity is enforced at the **data layer**, not the client:
+
+- **TMDB allowlist** — titles are validated against TMDB identity (see issue #128 /
+  title-copy-identity split, ADR 0002).
+- **Content checks** at ingest, at the same layer that owns the catalogue.
+
+This puts integrity next to the data it protects and covers every client uniformly.
+
+## Repository state
+
+Grep for `fingerprint`, `canvas`, `deviceId`, `user-agent` hashing, and
+`navigator.userAgent` found **no fingerprinting code**. The only `deviceId` usage is
+in the downloads subsystem (`server/src/main/kotlin/downloads/*`), where it is a
+client-supplied key for organizing per-device MP4 downloads — unrelated to
+fingerprinting. **Nothing to remove.**
+
+## Consequences
+
+- No fingerprinting is added now or planned.
+- Sybil/integrity concerns route to the data-layer controls above; revisit there,
+  not in the web client, if they resurface.
=== PR meta ===

toolUseResult.stdout:
diff --git a/docs/adr/p2p-0012-status.md b/docs/adr/p2p-0012-status.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..5fc352d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/adr/p2p-0012-status.md
@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+---
+status: rejected
+issue: viewrr/viewrr#129
+p2p-adr: 0012
+milestone: P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture
+---
+
+# No device/browser fingerprinting for Sybil resistance (rejected)
+
+Device/browser fingerprinting (canvas hashing, user-agent hashing, `navigator`
+entropy, `deviceId` derivation) as a Sybil-resistance mechanism is **rejected**.
+This is a decision record, not a feature — nothing is built.
+
+## Why rejected
+
+- **Contradicts permissionless identity.** The P2P self-custody re-architecture is
+  built on identity that anyone can hold without gatekeeping. Fingerprinting is a
+  covert per-device gate — the opposite of permissionless.
+- **Privacy-hostile.** Silent client fingerprinting tracks users without consent and
+  is exactly the surveillance pattern a self-custody media server exists to avoid.
+- **Only touches the web tier.** Fingerprinting is a browser-side signal; native
+  clients (AFinity/CMP mobile, TV) never produce it. A defense that only covers one
+  of three surfaces is not a defense.
+- **Evadable.** Fingerprints are trivially spoofed/rotated (headless browsers,
+  UA overrides, VMs). It raises effort for honest users far more than for a Sybil
+  attacker.
+
+## Alternative (where integrity actually lives)
+
+Catalogue integrity is enforced at the **data layer**, not the client:
+
+- **TMDB allowlist** — titles are validated against TMDB identity (see issue #128 /
+  title-copy-identity split, ADR 0002).
+- **Content checks** at ingest, at the same layer that owns the catalogue.
+
+This puts integrity next to the data it protects and covers every client uniformly.
+
+## Repository state
+
+Grep for `fingerprint`, `canvas`, `deviceId`, `user-agent` hashing, and
+`navigator.userAgent` found **no fingerprinting code**. The only `deviceId` usage is
+in the downloads subsystem (`server/src/main/kotlin/downloads/*`), where it is a
+client-supplied key for organizing per-device MP4 downloads — unrelated to
+fingerprinting. **Nothing to remove.**
+
+## Consequences
+
+- No fingerprinting is added now or planned.
+- Sybil/integrity concerns route to the data-layer controls above; revisit there,
+  not in the web client, if they resurface.

<result>Done. PR open against `main`, milestone attached, issue #128 left open.

## Files touched
- `server/src/main/resources/db/migration/V14__deindex_moderation.sql` (new) — Flyway migration
- `server/src/main/kotlin/db/Tables.kt` — `deindexed` column on `MediaItems`
- `server/src/main/kotlin/media/MediaModeration.kt` (new) — canonical rule + `publicCatalogOp()`
- `server/src/main/kotlin/media/MediaListRoutes.kt` — `/media` browse filter
- `server/src/main/kotlin/media/MediaSearchService.kt` — BM25 search WHERE
- `server/src/main/kotlin/media/HomeRoutes.kt` — featured/recent/byIds filters
- `server/src/main/kotlin/stremio/StremioService.kt` — movie/series catalog filters
- `server/src/main/kotlin/scanner/MediaAdminRoutes.kt` — `POST /admin/media/{id}/deindex`
- `server/src/test/kotlin/media/MediaModerationTest.kt` (new)

## Migration + filter
```sql
ALTER TABLE media_items ADD COLUMN deindexed BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false;
```
Rule (one predicate, reused everywhere): `!deindexed && tmdbId != null`.
- Exposed: `(MediaItems.deindexed eq false) and MediaItems.tmdbId.isNotNull()` via `publicCatalogOp()`
- Raw BM25 SQL: `AND deindexed = false AND tmdb_id IS NOT NULL`

Applied to discovery surfaces only (browse, search, home rows, Stremio catalog). By-id detail/playback intentionally not gated — hides from catalog without deleting, matching the issue.

## Test result
`./gradlew :server:test` → BUILD SUCCESSFUL. `MediaModerationTest`: tests=4, failures=0. Full server suite green. (Note: no live Postgres/ParadeDB in the test harness — the self-check asserts the canonical predicate the SQL mirrors, not an integration DB query.)

## PR
https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/pull/134

## Honest completion status
Complete and shippable for the two capabilities as specified. One flagged item needs human confirmation (in the PR body, not blocking): the TMDB allowlist gate will make existing never-TMDB-matched Titles vanish from `/media` browse once V14 ships. That's the literal reading of the issue and correct for a shared/public catalog, but if `/media` is meant to stay the owner's private full-library view, the gate on that one endpoint should be reconsidered. Playback (by id) is unaffected. Marking that decision **needs-human**.</result>

=== MediaModeration.kt ===
package wtf.jobin.media

import org.jetbrains.exposed.v1.core.*
import wtf.jobin.db.MediaItems

/**
 * #128 (P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture) — De-index-only moderation + TMDB allowlist.
 *
 * A Title is "publicly indexable" — eligible to appear in the central catalog's
 * DISCOVERY surfaces (browse `/media`, `/media/search`, the `/home` rows, Stremio catalog) —
 * only when BOTH hold:
 *   1. it has NOT been operator de-indexed (`media_items.deindexed = false`), and
 *   2. it has a TMDB match (`tmdb_id IS NOT NULL`) — the allowlist gate; a Title
 *      with no tmdbId is private-by-default and is never surfaced.
 *
 * De-index HIDES; it never deletes. Direct-by-id detail (`/media/{id}`), playback,
 * Stremio stream/meta resolution and owner/admin tools intentionally do NOT apply
 * this gate — the caller already holds the id, so it is not a discovery surface.
 *
 * ponytail: one boolean column + one reused predicate — deliberately NOT a
 * moderation subsystem. [isPubliclyIndexable] is the canonical, unit-tested rule;
 * [publicCatalogOp] is its SQL mirror used by the Exposed discovery queries, and
 * the raw BM25 WHERE clause in [MediaSearchService] mirrors it too — all three MUST
 * stay in sync. Upgrade path if moderation grows reason/actor/audit: a blocklist
 * table keyed by (tmdbId | mediaItemId) + a partial index. YAGNI until then.
 */
object MediaModeration {
    /** Canonical visibility rule. The SQL surfaces mirror exactly this. */
    fun isPubliclyIndexable(deindexed: Boolean, tmdbId: Int?): Boolean =
        !deindexed && tmdbId != null
}

/**
 * Exposed predicate mirroring [MediaModeration.isPubliclyIndexable], for query-side
 * (in-DB) filtering of the public discovery surfaces. Compose with `and`:
 * `MediaItems.selectAll().where { MediaItems.showTitle.isNull() and publicCatalogOp() }`.
 */
fun publicCatalogOp(): Op<Boolean> =
    (MediaItems.deindexed eq false) and MediaItems.tmdbId.isNotNull()
=== V14 migration ===
-- #128 (P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture): De-index-only moderation + TMDB allowlist.
--
-- deindexed: operator blocklist flag. When true the Title is hidden from every
-- PUBLIC DISCOVERY surface (browse, search, home rows, Stremio catalog) but the
-- row and its physical Copies are NOT deleted — direct-by-id detail/playback and
-- owner/admin tools still resolve it. De-index HIDES; it never removes bytes.
--
-- TMDB allowlist gate: reuses the existing tmdb_id column (V9). A Title with
-- tmdb_id IS NULL is private-by-default and never surfaced in the public catalog.
-- No new column is needed for the allowlist — the gate is (tmdb_id IS NOT NULL).
--
-- ponytail: one boolean, DEFAULT false, so every existing Title keeps its prior
-- visibility (still subject to the tmdb_id allowlist gate). No partial index yet —
-- personal libraries are small and each discovery query filters in-DB. If the
-- catalog ever grows enough to matter, add:
--   CREATE INDEX media_items_public_idx ON media_items (created_at)
--     WHERE deindexed = false AND tmdb_id IS NOT NULL;
-- Upgrade path if moderation needs reason/actor/audit: promote to a blocklist
-- table keyed by (tmdb_id | media_item_id). YAGNI until then.
ALTER TABLE media_items ADD COLUMN deindexed BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false;

content.text:
Reviewed PR #134 — code is genuinely good: one canonical predicate (`!deindexed && tmdbId != null`) mirrored across Exposed + raw BM25, backward-safe migration (`DEFAULT false`), by-id/playback correctly *not* gated, admin de-index endpoint, 4 passing tests, ponytail comments naming the upgrade path. Merge-ready **except** the one thing the agent honestly flagged — so I'm **not** closing #128.

**The needs-human call:** the TMDB *allowlist* gate is applied to **owner surfaces** too — `/media` browse (`MediaListRoutes:72`), `/home` rows, and search — not just the public Stremio catalog. Effect: once V14 ships, **any title the owner has that never matched TMDB (home videos, obscure rips) vanishes from their own browse/search/home.**

- **De-index blocklist** (explicit operator hide) → correct on all surfaces. No question.
- **TMDB allowlist auto-gate** → correct for a *public/shared* catalog, wrong for the *owner's library view*.

Our own glossary says: **public catalog = TMDB-allowlisted; private stash = everything.** The code doesn't yet have that split, so the agent applied the public rule everywhere.

**Recommendation (B):** keep de-index on all discovery surfaces; apply the **TMDB gate only to the genuinely public surface — the Stremio catalog** — and drop `tmdbId` from the gate on owner browse/`/home`/search (de-index-only there). Owners keep sight of everything they own; the shared catalog stays TMDB-clean.

**Alternative (A):** keep gate-all — accept that in the P2P direction everything trends toward the public catalog, so owners must TMDB-match to surface their own content.

Which — **A (gate-all, close #128 now)** or **B (split: TMDB-gate Stremio only)**? If B, I resume the #128 agent to adjust the predicate, then close. #128 stays open till you pick.

content.text: B. Resuming the #128 agent to split the predicate — de-index everywhere, TMDB gate on Stremio only.

input.summary: Split gate: TMDB on Stremio only
input.message:
Decision on your needs-human flag: **B — split the gate.** Owners must keep seeing their own non-TMDB titles; only the public Stremio catalog gets the TMDB allowlist.

Adjust in your same worktree/branch (`p2p/128-deindex`, PR #134):

1. **Split the predicate into two:**
   - `publicCatalogOp()` = `deindexed = false AND tmdbId IS NOT NULL` — **Stremio catalog only** (the public/shared surface).
   - New `notDeindexedOp()` (or similar) = `deindexed = false` **only** — used by the OWNER surfaces: `/media` browse (MediaListRoutes), `/home` rows (HomeRoutes), and search.
2. **MediaSearchService** raw BM25 WHERE: drop `AND tmdb_id IS NOT NULL`; KEEP `AND deindexed = false`.
3. **StremioService**: keep using `publicCatalogOp()` (full gate) — unchanged.
4. **MediaModeration.kt**: keep `isPubliclyIndexable(deindexed, tmdbId)` as the Stremio/public rule; add a second canonical fn for owner surfaces (`isVisibleToOwner(deindexed) = !deindexed`) and update the doc comment to state the split clearly (public catalog = TMDB-allowlisted; owner surfaces = de-index-only). Keep the "three mirrors stay in sync" note.
5. **Tests**: update/extend MediaModerationTest to cover BOTH rules — a non-TMDB, non-deindexed title is `isVisibleToOwner = true` but `isPubliclyIndexable = false`; a de-indexed title is false for both.
6. Run `./gradlew :server:test`, confirm green. Commit (msg refs #128, notes the owner/public split), push to the same branch so PR #134 updates. 

Reply with: files changed, test result, and confirm PR #134 updated. Do NOT close the issue — I'll close after verifying.

input.description: Issue #120 identity foundation
input.prompt:
You are working in a git worktree of the `viewrr/viewrr` repo — the Kotlin/Ktor SERVER of a self-hosted media platform (JVM 21, Exposed ORM, Flyway migrations, ParadeDB/Postgres). Your ONLY working directory:

REDACTED

cd there first. Branch `p2p/120-identity` is already created off origin/main.

ISSUE viewrr/viewrr#120 "[P2P] Replace Keycloak/OAuth with mnemonic-derived self-custody identity" (P2P-ADR 0001). The full issue is a large migration. You are building ONE bounded, honest FIRST INCREMENT — the SERVER-SIDE identity seam only. Do NOT attempt the whole thing.

SCOPE (this increment, and nothing beyond it):
1. **Accounts-by-publicKey:** an Exposed table + Flyway migration (next version after V14 — check existing `db/migration/`, likely V15) for accounts keyed by an Ed25519 `public_key` (BYTEA/hex), UNIQUE. Minimal columns (publicKey, created_at).
2. **Register endpoint:** `POST /identity/register` body `{ publicKey, signature }` where signature = Ed25519 sign of the literal `"viewrr:register"` by the user's secret key. Verify the signature, then insert the account (idempotent: existing publicKey → 200, no dup).
3. **Challenge-response auth:** `GET /identity/challenge` returns a random nonce; `POST /identity/verify` body `{ publicKey, signature }` over that nonce → verify → issue whatever session/token the app already uses (REUSE the existing session/JWT mechanism if one exists — find it; do NOT invent a new one). This proves publicKey ownership without Keycloak.
4. **Ed25519 verification util:** use **JDK-native `java.security`** (`Signature.getInstance("Ed25519")`, `KeyFactory`/`X509EncodedKeySpec` or raw 32-byte key handling). **NO Bouncy Castle. NO new crypto dependency.** JVM is 21 — native Ed25519 is available.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- Do NOT remove or break Keycloak/existing auth — ADD this path alongside. Retiring Keycloak (#112/#114/#115 already closed) is a SEPARATE follow-up, not this increment.
- The client-side mnemonic→keypair UI lives in other repos (mobile/web) — OUT of scope. Server only.
- ZERO new dependencies. Reuse Exposed, Ktor, existing routing/session patterns, Kotlin stdlib. NO Bouncy Castle.

ETHOS:
- CAVEMAN: terse reporting. Code/commits normal.
- PONYTAIL: smallest honest increment. Reuse existing patterns. `ponytail:` comments on deliberate simplifications + upgrade path. No speculative abstractions.

DELIVERABLE:
- Implement the above. Add tests: Ed25519 verify happy/sad path, register (new + idempotent), challenge→verify happy/sad. Run `./gradlew :server:test`, confirm green.
- Commit (msg refs #120, notes "foundation increment 1: server-side publicKey identity + Ed25519 challenge auth; Keycloak retirement + client mnemonic are follow-ups").
- Push branch `p2p/120-identity`, open a **DRAFT** PR against `main` (`gh pr create --draft`), milestone "P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture", referencing #120. Do NOT close the issue.

RETURN (concise): files added, the Ed25519 approach (confirm JDK-native, no BC), test result, PR URL, and an honest status — what this increment covers and what remains for #120 (Keycloak retirement, client mnemonic flow, Noise transport auth). gh is authenticated. Stay inside your worktree dir only.

<result>Done. Increment complete, tests green, draft PR open.

## Files added (all under `server/`)
**Main (`src/main/kotlin/identity/`)**
- `Ed25519Verifier.kt` — JDK-native verify util
- `IdentityModels.kt` — request/response DTOs
- `IdentityAccountRepository.kt` — Exposed repo (find/create by publicKey)
- `ChallengeStore.kt` — `ChallengeStore` interface + `RedisChallengeStore` (single-use nonce)
- `IdentityService.kt` — register / challenge / verify logic + `IdentityError`
- `IdentityRoutes.kt` — `/identity/{register,challenge,verify}`

**Schema / wiring (edits)**
- `resources/db/migration/V15__identity_accounts.sql` — new table
- `db/Tables.kt` — `IdentityAccounts` Exposed mirror
- `koin/Modules.kt` + `Koin.kt` — `identityModule` (reuses TokenService + Redis)
- `Routing.kt` — mounts `identityRoutes` alongside `authRoutes`

**Tests (`src/test/kotlin/identity/`)**
- `Ed25519TestIdentity.kt`, `Ed25519VerifierTest.kt`, `IdentityServiceTest.kt`

## Ed25519 approach — confirmed JDK-native, no BC
`java.security` only: `Signature.getInstance("Ed25519")` + `KeyFactory` decoding the raw 32-byte key wrapped in the fixed Ed25519 SPKI DER prefix via `X509EncodedKeySpec`. **No Bouncy Castle, zero new dependencies** (JVM 21, JEP 339).

## Test result
`./gradlew :server:test` → **BUILD SUCCESSFUL, 37 tests, 0 failures.** Coverage: Ed25519 verify happy/tampered/wrong-key/wrong-msg/malformed; register new+idempotent+bad-sig; challenge→verify happy/replay/bad-sig/unknown-challenge/unregistered-key.

## PR
Draft: **https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/pull/135** — base `main`, milestone "P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture", refs #120. Issue **not** closed.

## Honest status
**This increment covers:** the server-side identity seam only — an account table keyed by Ed25519 publicKey, a register endpoint (signature-gated, idempotent), and challenge-response auth that reuses the app's *existing* JWT/refresh session (`TokenService`) — proving key ownership without Keycloak. It runs strictly **alongside** Keycloak; nothing existing was removed.

**Deliberate simplifications (ponytail-noted in code):** publicKey stored as hex TEXT not BYTEA; identities are non-admin (no key→admin path yet); `/identity/verify` echoes the `challenge` in its body (vs the issue's bare `{publicKey,signature}` sketch) so the nonce can be bound + single-use-consumed statelessly.

**Remaining for #120 (separate follow-ups, not touched here):** Keycloak/OAuth retirement (#112/#114/#115), the client mnemonic→keypair UI (mobile/web repos), and Noise transport auth for the P2P layer.

One caveat worth flagging: the register/verify endpoints and the Exposed repo compile and are unit-tested with fakes, but the actual Postgres round-trip (real `insertAndGetId` against `identity_accounts`) and the live Redis nonce path are **not** exercised by an integration test in this increment — the repo suite is pure-unit by existing convention. That's the honest ceiling on "green."</result>

## Files added (all under `server/`)
**Main (`src/main/kotlin/identity/`)**
- `Ed25519Verifier.kt` — JDK-native verify util
- `IdentityModels.kt` — request/response DTOs
- `IdentityAccountRepository.kt` — Exposed repo (find/create by publicKey)
- `ChallengeStore.kt` — `ChallengeStore` interface + `RedisChallengeStore` (single-use nonce)
- `IdentityService.kt` — register / challenge / verify logic + `IdentityError`
- `IdentityRoutes.kt` — `/identity/{register,challenge,verify}`

**Schema / wiring (edits)**
- `resources/db/migration/V15__identity_accounts.sql` — new table
- `db/Tables.kt` — `IdentityAccounts` Exposed mirror
- `koin/Modules.kt` + `Koin.kt` — `identityModule` (reuses TokenService + Redis)
- `Routing.kt` — mounts `identityRoutes` alongside `authRoutes`

**Tests (`src/test/kotlin/identity/`)**
- `Ed25519TestIdentity.kt`, `Ed25519VerifierTest.kt`, `IdentityServiceTest.kt`

## Ed25519 approach — confirmed JDK-native, no BC
`java.security` only: `Signature.getInstance("Ed25519")` + `KeyFactory` decoding the raw 32-byte key wrapped in the fixed Ed25519 SPKI DER prefix via `X509EncodedKeySpec`. **No Bouncy Castle, zero new dependencies** (JVM 21, JEP 339).

## Test result
`./gradlew :server:test` → **BUILD SUCCESSFUL, 37 tests, 0 failures.** Coverage: Ed25519 verify happy/tampered/wrong-key/wrong-msg/malformed; register new+idempotent+bad-sig; challenge→verify happy/replay/bad-sig/unknown-challenge/unregistered-key.

## PR
Draft: **https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/pull/135** — base `main`, milestone "P2P / Self-Custody Re-architecture", refs #120. Issue **not** closed.

## Honest status
**This increment covers:** the server-side identity seam only — an account table keyed by Ed25519 publicKey, a register endpoint (signature-gated, idempotent), and challenge-response auth that reuses the app's *existing* JWT/refresh session (`TokenService`) — proving key ownership without Keycloak. It runs strictly **alongside** Keycloak; nothing existing was removed.

**Deliberate simplifications (ponytail-noted in code):** publicKey stored as hex TEXT not BYTEA; identities are non-admin (no key→admin path yet); `/identity/verify` echoes the `challenge` in its body (vs the issue's bare `{publicKey,signature}` sketch) so the nonce can be bound + single-use-consumed statelessly.

**Remaining for #120 (separate follow-ups, not touched here):** Keycloak/OAuth retirement (#112/#114/#115), the client mnemonic→keypair UI (mobile/web repos), and Noise transport auth for the P2P layer.

One caveat worth flagging: the register/verify endpoints and the Exposed repo compile and are unit-tested with fakes, but the actual Postgres round-trip (real `insertAndGetId` against `identity_accounts`) and the live Redis nonce path are **not** exercised by an integration test in this increment — the repo suite is pure-unit by existing convention. content.text: #120 increment landed — draft PR #135, 37 tests green. Spot-reviewing: confirming JDK-native Ed25519, zero new deps, no Bouncy Castle.

input.command:
WT="REDACTED"
echo "=== Bouncy Castle anywhere? (want none) ==="; grep -rniE "bouncy ?castle|org\.bouncycastle" "$WT/server" 2>/dev/null || echo "  none ✓"
echo "=== new deps added to build.gradle? (want empty diff) ==="; git -C "$WT" diff origin/main -- server/build.gradle.kts | grep -E "^\+.*(implementation|api)\(" || echo "  no dependency lines added ✓"
echo "=== Ed25519Verifier imports ==="; grep -n "^import" "$WT/server/src/main/kotlin/identity/Ed25519Verifier.kt"
echo "=== V15 migration ==="; sed -n '1,20p' "$WT/server/src/main/resources/db/migration/V15__identity_accounts.sql"
echo "=== Keycloak/authRoutes still mounted? ==="; grep -n "authRoutes\|identityRoutes" "$WT/server/src/main/kotlin/Routing.kt"
input.description: Verify no BC, no new deps, native Ed25519, Keycloak intact

content.content:
=== Bouncy Castle anywhere? (want none) ===
REDACTED.kt:9: * JVM 21). NO Bouncy Castle, NO third-party crypto dependency — the whole point of #120's
=== new deps added to build.gradle? (want empty diff) ===
  no dependency lines added ✓
=== Ed25519Verifier imports ===
3:import java.security.KeyFactory
4:import java.security.Signature
5:import java.security.spec.X509EncodedKeySpec
=== V15 migration ===
-- #120 (P2P-ADR 0001), foundation increment 1: self-custody identity keyed by an
-- Ed25519 public key. Runs ALONGSIDE the existing users/Keycloak auth — nothing here
-- retires Keycloak (that is #112/#114/#115 follow-up). The client mnemonic->keypair flow
-- lives in the mobile/web repos; the Hub only ever sees the public key.
--
-- ponytail: public_key is stored as lowercase hex TEXT, not BYTEA. Ed25519 raw keys are
-- 32 bytes -> 64 hex chars; TEXT keeps the Exposed mirror trivial and the UNIQUE index
-- canonical (callers normalize to lowercase before insert). Switch to BYTEA only if the
-- account count ever makes 2x storage matter — it won't for a self-hosted media Hub.
CREATE TABLE identity_accounts (
    id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    public_key  TEXT        NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
=== Keycloak/authRoutes still mounted? ===
12:import wtf.jobin.auth.authRoutes
14:import wtf.jobin.identity.identityRoutes
100:        authRoutes(auth)
101:        identityRoutes(identity)

content.text:

content.text: Before building this, let me see what that feed actually contains — then I have a ponytail-correct concern about the approach.

input.prompt: What does this JSON feed contain? List the top-level fields and the fields of each item. Is it movie reviews? Is there any numeric rating/score/star value per item, or just article title/url/description/date? Give 2 example item titles. Does anything tie an item to a specific movie (e.g. a movie title, IMDb/TMDB id)?

lastPrompt: use https://rss.app/feeds/v1.1/XuBRUTnUaoC0nSUx.json for adding reviews and look for other review site rss feeds to fetch reviews of movie and display star rating icons in the movie thumbnail

content.content:
## JSON Feed Structure

**Top-level fields:** `version`, `title`, `home_page_url`, `feed_url`, `favicon`, `language`, `description`, `items`

**Fields per item:** `id`, `url`, `title`, `content_text`, `content_html`, `image`, `date_published`, `authors` (with `name`), `attachments` (with `url`)

## Is it movie reviews?

Mostly no — it's a mix of movie news. The feed title is "Movies | Hollywood Reporter." Most entries are industry news (casting, deals, festivals, obituaries, trailers), with only a few actual reviews (URLs containing `movie-reviews`).

## Numeric rating/score/star value?

**No.** There is no rating, score, or star field in any item. Each item contains only title, url, description text, image, publish date, and author — no structured numeric evaluation.

## Example item titles

1. "'Enola Holmes 3' Review: Millie Bobby Brown in Netflix Threequel"
2. "'Minions & Monsters' Review: A Clever Mix of Silliness and Cinephilia"

## Does anything tie an item to a specific movie?

Only loosely, and only via **text** — there is **no structured movie identifier** (no IMDb/TMDB id, no dedicated movie-title field). Movie titles appear informally inside the `title`, `url` slug, and `content_text`. For example, one item references "Ocean's Eleven Prequel" and another "Enola Holmes 3," but these are just words in the headline/description, not machine-readable references.

content.text:
Fetched the feed — key finding that changes the approach: it's **Hollywood Reporter "Movies"** = industry news + a few reviews. **No ratings, no scores, no movie IDs.** Items are just article title/url/text/image/date, with movie names only as loose text ("'Enola Holmes 3' Review…").

That splits your ask into two things with **different right sources** — and RSS is the wrong tool for one of them:

**1. Star rating on the thumbnail → use TMDB, not RSS.**
viewrr already fetches TMDB per title (`tmdbId` matching, #82). **TMDB gives `vote_average` (0–10) + vote_count** already. Stars = `vote_average / 2`. RSS feeds carry *no* numeric rating, so scraping them for stars is building fragile machinery for a number you already hold. For multi-site scores (IMDb/RT/Metacritic) the clean source is **OMDb API** (one call by imdbId), still not RSS. So: **stars come from TMDB (already in hand); zero RSS needed.**

**2. Editorial highlights (reviews, Oscar/Globe noms, festival wins) → RSS, but best-effort.**
*This* is what RSS is actually for — news streams. The THR feed + others carry "X Review", "Oscar nominations", "Cannes winner" as articles. The catch: **no movie IDs**, so tying an article to a catalog title = **fuzzy title matching** ("'Enola Holmes 3' Review" → your "Enola Holmes 3"). Works, but imperfect — false matches + misses. It's a "highlight badge" feature, not authoritative.

**Proposed build (confirm before I start):**
- **Stars** ← TMDB `vote_average` → server exposes it on `media_items`, client renders star icons on the thumbnail. *(client = mobile/web repos, separate from this Ktor server.)*
- **Highlight badges** ← poll N RSS feeds, keyword-classify (`review` / `oscar-nom` / `globe-nom` / `festival-win`), fuzzy-match to catalog titles, attach a badge ("Oscar nominee", "Cannes winner", "Reviewed"). THR (yours) + candidates: **Variety, Deadline, IndieWire, RogerEbert.com** (reviews); **GoldDerby / AwardsWatch** (awards); **Cannes/Venice/Sundance** feeds (festivals).

Two things to confirm:
1. **Stars from TMDB, not RSS** — agree? (It's the only real rating source; RSS has none.)
2. **Badges are best-effort fuzzy matches** — accept that, or do you want a stricter source for awards (Wikidata/IMDb structured data, heavier)?

Confirm the split and I'll build the server side (rating field + RSS ingest + fuzzy matcher + badge table), then hand the thumbnail rendering to the client repos.

content.text:
Confirmed. Those outlets are all the **reviews** layer — same RSS ingest, different from stars/badges. Honest per-outlet reality:

- **Have usable feeds:** Hollywood Reporter (your rss.app JSON), NYT Movies, Washington Post, Rediff, Times of India, Film Companion (WordPress `/feed`), + Variety/IndieWire/RogerEbert.
- **Paywalled (NYT, WaPo):** RSS gives headline + link only → we **link out** ("Read at NYT"), no full text.
- **No uniform star number across them** — Rediff/TOI publish in-text ratings (parseable, fragile); NYT/Film Companion don't rate. So per-outlet = a **review link** (+ parsed rating only where the outlet gives one). The single thumbnail star stays **TMDB** (the one consistent source).
- **"Lensman" — unknown to me.** Confirm the exact site/URL and I'll add it.

**Locked design (one subsystem, config-driven):**
- **Thumbnail:** TMDB stars + optionally one award badge. *(client repos render it.)*
- **Detail page:** list of critic-review links (outlet + link + date + optional rating) + award/festival badges. Reviews don't fit a thumbnail.
- **Server:** one "editorial ingest" — a **configurable feed list** → poll → classify (review / oscar-nom / globe-nom / festival-win) → fuzzy title-match to catalog → store as review-link or badge. Adding an outlet = config, not code. Normalize every feed to JSON Feed (rss.app-style) or parse RSS/Atom with **JDK `javax.xml`** — no new dependency.

Reviews → detail, not thumbnail (a link list can't live on a thumbnail). Building on that assumption. Kicking off the server subsystem now — agent verifies each feed URL as it wires them.

content.text: Deps live in a version catalog, not inline — the agent will discover. Spawning the editorial-ingest agent (config-driven, JDK-native parsing, zero new deps, best-effort fuzzy matching, reviews→detail + stars/badges).

input.description: Editorial reviews ingest subsystem
input.prompt:
You are building a NEW feature in the `viewrr/viewrr` Kotlin/Ktor SERVER (JVM 21, Exposed ORM, Flyway migrations, Postgres/ParadeDB). This is catalog enrichment — orthogonal to the P2P epic, builds on the shipped architecture. Your ONLY working directory:

REDACTED

cd there first. Branch `feat/editorial-reviews` is created off origin/main.

GOAL — an "editorial ingest" subsystem that enriches catalog movies with (a) critic REVIEW links, (b) award/festival HIGHLIGHT badges, and exposes (c) a TMDB star rating. Client repos render stars+badge on the thumbnail and the review list on the detail page — YOU build the SERVER only.

BUILD (config-driven, smallest honest version):
1. **Feed config** — a list of outlet feeds {name, url, format: json|xml, type: reviews|awards|festival}. Adding an outlet = editing config, NOT code. Seed it and VERIFY each URL actually resolves + is a feed before including (use WebFetch to check reachability/shape only — do NOT dump full bodies). Candidates to verify: Hollywood Reporter JSON `https://rss.app/feeds/v1.1/XuBRUTnUaoC0nSUx.json` (KNOWN GOOD); NYT Movies `https://rss.nytimes.com/services/xml/rss/nyt/Movies.xml`; Washington Post entertainment/movies RSS; Rediff movies RSS; Times of India movie-reviews RSS; Film Companion `https://www.filmcompanion.in/feed`; Variety, IndieWire, RogerEbert.com reviews. Include only ones that resolve; comment out dead ones with a note. **"Lensman" is an unknown outlet — flag it in your report, don't guess a URL.**
2. **Fetcher** — JSON Feed v1.1 via the app's existing kotlinx.serialization (reuse it). RSS/Atom XML via **JDK `javax.xml`** (native). HTTP via the app's existing Ktor client if present, else **`java.net.http.HttpClient`** (JDK native). ZERO new dependencies — if something seems to need one, STOP and report instead of adding it.
3. **Classifier** — keyword rules on item title/content → `review` | `oscar-nom` | `globe-nom` | `festival-win` | ignore. (Academy Award/Oscar + nominat*, Golden Globe + nominat*, Cannes/Venice/Sundance/Berlin/Toronto + win|winner|opening|premiere.)
4. **Fuzzy matcher** — extract the movie-title mention from an item and match it to `media_items.title` (+ year when present). Normalized-string + token-overlap scoring, no new dep. Only attach above a confidence threshold; store the score. Best-effort by design.
5. **Storage** (Flyway migration, next version after existing — check `db/migration/`, likely V16) — `movie_reviews`(media_item_id, outlet, url, published_at, snippet, parsed_rating NULLABLE) + `movie_highlights`(media_item_id, type, label, source_url, date). Exposed mirrors in `db/Tables.kt`.
6. **Star rating** — expose TMDB `vote_average` (0-10) + vote_count on the media read endpoint. Check whether it is already stored on media_items / fetched during tmdbId matching (#82); if stored, expose it; if not, note where it comes from. Server returns the number; client maps to 5 stars.
7. **Endpoints** — `GET /media/{id}/reviews` → { reviews:[...], highlights:[...] }. Extend the existing media-detail response if that's cleaner.
8. **Refresh** — a manual admin trigger `POST /admin/editorial/refresh` (+ an OPTIONAL interval if the repo already has a scheduler pattern — reuse it; do NOT build a cron framework). ponytail: manual/admin trigger is enough for v1.
9. **Tests** — classifier keyword cases; fuzzy matcher match/no-match; parse one JSON feed sample + one XML RSS sample (use small inline fixtures, no live network in tests); the reviews endpoint. `./gradlew :server:test` green.

ETHOS: CAVEMAN reporting (terse). PONYTAIL: smallest honest build, reuse existing patterns/deps, JDK-native over new libs, config over hardcode, `ponytail:` comments on simplifications. No speculative abstractions.

DELIVERABLE: implement; tests green; commit (msg: "feat: editorial ingest — critic review links + award/festival badges + TMDB star exposure"); push `feat/editorial-reviews`; open a PR (normal, not draft) against `main`. No milestone (not part of the P2P epic). 

RETURN (concise): feeds verified-live vs dead (+ Lensman flagged), tables+endpoints added, where the TMDB star number comes from, whether any dep was needed (should be none), test result, PR URL, and honest ceiling (fuzzy-match accuracy, paywalled outlets = link-only). gh is authenticated. Stay in your worktree dir only.

6. **Star rating** — expose TMDB `vote_average` (0-10) + vote_count on the media read endpoint. Check whether it is already stored on media_items / fetched during tmdbId matching (#82); if stored, expose it; if not, note where it comes from. Server returns the number; client maps to 5 stars.
7. **Endpoints** — `GET /media/{id}/reviews` → { reviews:[...], highlights:[...] }. Extend the existing media-detail response if that's cleaner.
8. **Refresh** — a manual admin trigger `POST /admin/editorial/refresh` (+ an OPTIONAL interval if the repo already has a scheduler pattern — reuse it; do NOT build a cron framework). ponytail: manual/admin trigger is enough for v1.
9. **Tests** — classifier keyword cases; fuzzy matcher match/no-match; parse one JSON feed sample + one XML RSS sample (use small inline fixtures, no live network in tests); the reviews endpoint. `./gradlew :server:test` green.

ETHOS: CAVEMAN reporting (terse). PONYTAIL: smallest honest build, reuse existing patterns/deps, JDK-native over new libs, config over hardcode, `ponytail:` comments on simplifications. No speculative abstractions.

DELIVERABLE: implement; tests green; commit (msg: "feat: editorial ingest — critic review links + award/festival badges + TMDB star exposure"); push `feat/editorial-reviews`; open a PR (normal, not draft) against `main`. No milestone (not part of the P2P epic). 

RETURN (concise): feeds verified-live vs dead (+ Lensman flagged), tables+endpoints added, where the TMDB star number comes from, whether any dep was needed (should be none), test result, PR URL, and honest ceiling (fuzzy-match accuracy, paywalled outlets = link-only). gh is authenticated. Stay in your worktree dir only.

input.description: Editorial reviews ingest subsystem
input.prompt:
You are building a NEW feature in the `viewrr/viewrr` Kotlin/Ktor SERVER (JVM 21, Exposed ORM, Flyway migrations, Postgres/ParadeDB). Catalog enrichment — orthogonal to the P2P epic, builds on the shipped architecture. Your ONLY working directory:

REDACTED

cd there first. Branch `feat/editorial-reviews` is on origin/main base with nothing built yet (a prior attempt died early — start clean).

GOAL — an "editorial ingest" subsystem enriching catalog movies with (a) critic REVIEW links, (b) award/festival HIGHLIGHT badges, and exposing (c) a TMDB star rating. Client repos render stars+badge on the thumbnail and the review list on the detail page — YOU build the SERVER only.

BUILD (config-driven, smallest honest version):
1. **Feed config** — list of {name, url, format: json|xml, type: reviews|awards|festival}. Adding an outlet = editing config, NOT code. Seed + VERIFY each URL resolves + is a feed before including (WebFetch reachability/shape only, do NOT dump bodies; if a fetch is slow/blocked, mark it unverified in a comment and move on — do NOT get stuck). Candidates: Hollywood Reporter JSON `https://rss.app/feeds/v1.1/XuBRUTnUaoC0nSUx.json` (KNOWN GOOD — include without re-verifying); NYT Movies `https://rss.nytimes.com/services/xml/rss/nyt/Movies.xml`; Washington Post movies RSS; Rediff movies RSS; Times of India movie-reviews RSS; Film Companion `https://www.filmcompanion.in/feed`; Variety, IndieWire, RogerEbert.com. Include ones that resolve; comment out dead/unverified with a note. **"Lensman" is unknown — flag in report, do not guess a URL.** Cap verification to a few minutes total; the config being extensible matters more than verifying every URL now.
2. **Fetcher** — JSON Feed v1.1 via existing kotlinx.serialization; RSS/Atom XML via JDK `javax.xml`; HTTP via existing Ktor client if present else `java.net.http.HttpClient`. ZERO new dependencies — if something seems to need one, STOP and report instead of adding.
3. **Classifier** — keyword rules on item title/content → `review` | `oscar-nom` | `globe-nom` | `festival-win` | ignore.
4. **Fuzzy matcher** — extract movie-title mention, match to `media_items.title` (+year when present), normalized-string + token-overlap, no new dep, confidence threshold, store score. Best-effort.
5. **Storage** (Flyway migration, next version after existing — check `db/migration/`, likely V16) — `movie_reviews`(media_item_id, outlet, url, published_at, snippet, parsed_rating NULLABLE) + `movie_highlights`(media_item_id, type, label, source_url, date). Exposed mirrors in `db/Tables.kt`.
6. **Star rating** — expose TMDB `vote_average`(0-10)+vote_count on the media read endpoint; check if already stored on media_items (tmdbId matching #82) — expose if stored, else note source.
7. **Endpoints** — `GET /media/{id}/reviews` → {reviews:[...], highlights:[...]}; extend existing media-detail response if cleaner.
8. **Refresh** — manual `POST /admin/editorial/refresh` (+ optional interval only if the repo already has a scheduler pattern to reuse; do NOT build a cron framework).
9. **Tests** — classifier cases; fuzzy matcher match/no-match; parse one JSON + one XML fixture (small inline, no live network); reviews endpoint. `./gradlew :server:test` green.

ETHOS: CAVEMAN terse reporting. PONYTAIL: smallest honest build, reuse existing patterns/deps, JDK-native over new libs, config over hardcode, `ponytail:` comments. No speculative abstractions.

DELIVERABLE: implement; tests green; commit ("feat: editorial ingest — critic review links + award/festival badges + TMDB star exposure"); push `feat/editorial-reviews`; open a normal PR against `main` (no milestone).

RETURN (concise): feeds live vs dead (+ Lensman flagged), tables+endpoints, TMDB star source, any dep needed (should be none), test result, PR URL, honest ceiling (fuzzy-match accuracy, paywalled = link-only). gh is authenticated. Stay in your worktree dir only. If you approach a resource/time limit, COMMIT and PUSH whatever compiles + passes so far and report progress rather than losing work.

content.text: Agent left **uncommitted, incomplete** work (new `editorial/` package, migration, wired into Koin/Routing/scanner/TMDB) and stopped mid-edit — no commit, no PR. Plus a collision it couldn't have known about: it named its migration **`V14__editorial.sql`, but `V14__deindex` just merged to main** (#128) — Flyway will break on two V14s. Resuming the agent to finish + fix the collision (it knows its own half-built code).

input.summary: Finish editorial: fix V14 collision, commit, PR
input.message:
You stopped mid-edit (on "the backfill route update block") with everything UNCOMMITTED — nothing pushed, no PR. Finish it now, in the same worktree (`wt-reviews`, branch `feat/editorial-reviews`).

CRITICAL FIX FIRST — migration collision:
- You created `server/src/main/resources/db/migration/V14__editorial.sql`. But `V14__deindex_moderation.sql` has since MERGED to main, and `V15` is reserved for the #120 identity work. **Rename your migration to `V16__editorial.sql`** (git mv) so Flyway doesn't see two V14s. Update any code/test referencing the old filename/version.

THEN:
1. Finish the half-done edit you were on (the backfill route update block) and any other incomplete wiring.
2. Make it COMPILE and pass tests: `./gradlew :server:test`. Fix whatever's broken from the mid-edit stop. If a test needs a small inline fixture, add it — no live network in tests.
3. Sanity-check your scope is intact: feed config (extensible, Lensman flagged), JSON+XML fetch (JDK-native, ZERO new deps — confirm you added none), classifier, fuzzy matcher, `movie_reviews` + `movie_highlights` tables, TMDB `vote_average` exposure, `GET /media/{id}/reviews`, admin refresh trigger.
4. Commit ("feat: editorial ingest — critic review links + award/festival badges + TMDB star exposure"), push `feat/editorial-reviews`, open a NORMAL PR against main (no milestone).

RETURN concise: confirm migration renamed to V16, feeds live vs dead (+ Lensman), tables/endpoints, any dep added (should be none), `./gradlew :server:test` result, PR URL, honest ceiling. If you near a limit again: COMMIT + PUSH whatever compiles first, then report — do not leave work uncommitted.
input.content: Finish + fix V14 collision, commit, push, PR.

<task-notification>
<task-id>a1750efc154f43051</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_01FbDAzrrKSj5rWVq2JbUuZD</tool-use-id>

<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Editorial reviews ingest subsystem" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Done. Base=main, milestone=null, tree clean. Everything shipped.

---

**EDITORIAL INGEST — SHIPPED**

**Migration:** renamed V14 → **V16__editorial.sql** (dodged the merged `V14__deindex_moderation` + reserved V15/#120). Code comments updated too.

**Feeds — live vs dead:**
- LIVE (verified reachable + feed-shaped): **Hollywood Reporter** (JSON, known-good), **RogerEbert.com**, **Film Companion**, **Rediff Movies**.
- DISABLED w/ notes in `feeds.json`: NYT + Times of India (host refused our fetch — unverified), Variety + IndieWire (Tollbit bot-wall, 307), Washington Post (403).
- **Lensman: FLAGGED** — named in brief, no URL known, not guessed. Left disabled with a note.
- Adding an outlet = editing `feeds.json`, not code.

**Tables:** `movie_reviews`(media_item_id, outlet, url, published_at, snippet, parsed_rating NULL, match_score) + `movie_highlights`(media_item_id, type, label, source_url, date). Idempotent via unique keys. Exposed mirrors in `db/Tables.kt`.

**Endpoints:** `GET /media/{id}/reviews` → `{reviews, highlights}` (authed); `POST /admin/editorial/refresh` (admin, manual — no cron framework, none to reuse).

**TMDB star:** was **not stored** before. `TmdbClient` now parses `vote_average`/`vote_count` → `media_items.tmdb_vote_average`/`tmdb_vote_count` (V16), populated on scan + `backfill-tmdb`, exposed on `GET /media` + `/media/{id}`.

**Deps added: ZERO.** JDK `java.net.http` + `javax.xml` DOM + existing kotlinx.serialization only. `build.gradle.kts` untouched.

**Tests:** `./gradlew :server:test` → **44 tests, 0 failures.** Classifier, fuzzy matcher (match/no-match + extraction), JSON+XML inline fixtures, config parse, H2 in-memory R2DBC repo test (store/dedup/read). No live network in tests.

**PR:** https://github.com/viewrr/viewrr/pull/136 (base `main`, no milestone).

**Honest ceiling:** fuzzy match is a heuristic — solid on `'Title' Review` headlines, misses creative phrasing, can't split same-title remakes without a year (tuned for precision, threshold 0.6). Paywalled/bot-walled outlets = link-only; most feeds carry no machine rating so `parsed_rating` is usually null. Refresh is manual — wire an external cron/arr hook for periodic ingest.

Key files: `server/src/main/kotlin/editorial/` (7 files), `server/src/main/resources/editorial/feeds.json`, `server/src/main/resources/db/migration/V16__editorial.sql`, `server/src/test/kotlin/editorial/` (5 test files).</result>
<usage><subagent_tokens>282325</subagent_tokens><tool_uses>24</tool_uses><duration_ms>435863</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

1	package wtf.jobin.editorial
2	
3	import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
4	import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
5	import kotlinx.serialization.SerialName
6	import kotlinx.serialization.Serializable
7	import kotlinx.serialization.json.Json
8	import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory
9	import org.w3c.dom.Element
10	import org.w3c.dom.Node
11	import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
12	import java.net.URI
13	import java.net.http.HttpClient
14	import java.net.http.HttpRequest
15	import java.net.http.HttpResponse
16	import java.time.Instant
17	import java.time.OffsetDateTime
18	import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter
19	import javax.xml.parsers.DocumentBuilderFactory
20	
21	private val log = LoggerFactory.getLogger("wtf.jobin.editorial.FeedFetcher")
22	
23	/** One normalized entry from any feed, format-agnostic. */
24	data class FeedItem(
25	    val title: String,
26	    val url: String?,
27	    val summary: String?,      // short snippet, HTML stripped
28	    val content: String?,      // fuller body for the classifier, HTML stripped
29	    val publishedAt: Instant?,
30	)
31	
32	// --- JSON Feed v1.1 (https://www.jsonfeed.org/version/1.1/) ---------------------------------------
33	@Serializable
34	private data class JsonFeedDoc(val items: List<JsonFeedItem> = emptyList())
35	
36	@Serializable
37	private data class JsonFeedItem(
38	    val url: String? = null,
39	    @SerialName("external_url") val externalUrl: String? = null,
40	    val title: String? = null,
41	    val summary: String? = null,
42	    @SerialName("content_text") val contentText: String? = null,
43	    @SerialName("content_html") val contentHtml: String? = null,
44	    @SerialName("date_published") val datePublished: String? = null,
45	)
46	
47	/**
48	 * Fetches and parses editorial feeds using only JDK + already-present deps:
49	 * HTTP via java.net.http.HttpClient (same as TmdbClient), JSON via kotlinx.serialization, XML via
50	 * javax.xml DOM. ZERO new gradle dependencies. Network errors are swallowed to an empty list so one
51	 * dead outlet never sinks a whole refresh.
52	 */
53	class FeedFetcher(
54	    private val http: HttpClient = HttpClient.newHttpClient(),
55	) {
56	    private val json = Json { ignoreUnknownKeys = true }
57	
58	    suspend fun fetch(feed: FeedConfig): List<FeedItem> {
59	        val body = try {
60	            withContext(Dispatchers.IO) {
61	                val req = HttpRequest.newBuilder(URI.create(feed.url))
62	                    .header("User-Agent", "viewrr-editorial/1.0") // some feeds 403 the default UA
63	                    .GET().build()
64	                val res = http.send(req, HttpResponse.BodyHandlers.ofString())
65	                if (res.statusCode() != 200) {
66	                    log.warn("editorial feed {} HTTP {}", feed.name, res.statusCode())
67	                    null
68	                } else {
69	                    res.body()
70	                }
71	            }
72	        } catch (e: Exception) {
73	            log.warn("editorial feed {} fetch failed: {}", feed.name, e.message)
74	            null
75	        } ?: return emptyList()
76	
77	        return try {
78	            when (feed.format) {
79	                FeedFormat.json -> parseJsonFeed(body)
80	                FeedFormat.xml -> parseXmlFeed(body)
81	            }
82	        } catch (e: Exception) {
83	            log.warn("editorial feed {} parse failed: {}", feed.name, e.message)
84	            emptyList()
85	        }
86	    }
87	
88	    fun parseJsonFeed(text: String): List<FeedItem> =
89	        json.decodeFromString<JsonFeedDoc>(text).items.mapNotNull { it ->
90	            val title = it.title?.trim().orEmpty()
91	            if (title.isBlank()) return@mapNotNull null
92	            FeedItem(
93	                title = title,
94	                url = it.url ?: it.externalUrl,
95	                summary = stripHtml(it.summary ?: it.contentText ?: it.contentHtml),
96	                content = stripHtml(it.contentHtml ?: it.contentText ?: it.summary),
97	                publishedAt = parseInstant(it.datePublished),
98	            )
99	        }
100	
101	    /** Handles RSS 2.0 (`<item>`) and Atom (`<entry>`) in one pass. */
102	    fun parseXmlFeed(text: String): List<FeedItem> {
103	        val doc = newSecureBuilder().parse(ByteArrayInputStream(text.toByteArray(Charsets.UTF_8)))
104	        doc.documentElement.normalize()
105	        val entries = buildList {
106	            addAll(doc.getElementsByTagName("item").asList())   // RSS
107	            addAll(doc.getElementsByTagName("entry").asList())  // Atom
108	        }
109	        return entries.mapNotNull { el ->
110	            val title = childText(el, "title")?.trim().orEmpty()
111	            if (title.isBlank()) return@mapNotNull null
112	            val body = childText(el, "description")   // RSS
113	                ?: childText(el, "content")           // Atom / content:encoded
114	                ?: childText(el, "summary")           // Atom
115	            FeedItem(
116	                title = title,
117	                url = linkOf(el),
118	                summary = stripHtml(body)?.take(500),
119	                content = stripHtml(body),
120	                publishedAt = parseInstant(childText(el, "pubDate") ?: childText(el, "published") ?: childText(el, "updated")),
121	            )
122	        }
123	    }
124	
125	    // --- helpers ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
126	
127	    private fun newSecureBuilder() = DocumentBuilderFactory.newInstance().apply {
128	        // Harden against XXE: feeds are untrusted input.
129	        setFeature("http://apache.org/xml/features/disallow-doctype-decl", true)
130	        setFeature("http://xml.org/sax/features/external-general-entities", false)
131	        setFeature("http://xml.org/sax/features/external-parameter-entities", false)
132	        isNamespaceAware = false
133	    }.newDocumentBuilder()
134	
135	    /** RSS `<link>text</link>` vs Atom `<link href=".."/>`. Prefer rel=alternate for Atom. */
136	    private fun linkOf(el: Element): String? {
137	        val links = el.getElementsByTagName("link")
138	        for (i in 0 until links.length) {
139	            val n = links.item(i) as? Element ?: continue
140	            val href = n.getAttribute("href")
141	            if (href.isNotBlank()) {
142	                val rel = n.getAttribute("rel")
143	                if (rel.isBlank() || rel == "alternate") return href
144	            } else if (n.textContent.isNotBlank()) {
145	                return n.textContent.trim()
146	            }
147	        }
148	        return childText(el, "guid")
149	    }
150	
151	    private fun childText(el: Element, tag: String): String? {
152	        val nodes = el.getElementsByTagName(tag)
153	        for (i in 0 until nodes.length) {
154	            val t = nodes.item(i).textContent?.trim()
155	            if (!t.isNullOrBlank()) return t
156	        }
157	        return null
158	    }
159	
160	    private fun org.w3c.dom.NodeList.asList(): List<Element> =
161	        (0 until length).mapNotNull { item(it) as? Element }.filter { it.nodeType == Node.ELEMENT_NODE }
162	}
163	
164	// --- shared, side-effect-free parsing utilities (also used by the classifier/matcher) ------------
165	
166	/** Strip HTML tags + decode the handful of entities that show up in feed bodies. */
167	fun stripHtml(s: String?): String? {
168	    if (s == null) return null
169	    val noTags = s.replace(Regex("<[^>]+>"), " ")
170	    val decoded = noTags
171	        .replace("&", "&").replace("<", "<").replace(">", ">")
172	        .replace(""", "\"").replace("'", "'").replace("'", "'").replace(" ", " ")
173	    val collapsed = decoded.replace(Regex("\\s+"), " ").trim()
174	    return collapsed.ifBlank { null }
175	}
176	
177	/** Lenient timestamp parse: RFC3339 (JSON Feed / Atom) then RFC1123 (RSS pubDate). null on failure. */
178	fun parseInstant(raw: String?): Instant? {
179	    if (raw.isNullOrBlank()) return null
180	    return runCatching { Instant.parse(raw) }.getOrNull()
181	        ?: runCatching { OffsetDateTime.parse(raw).toInstant() }.getOrNull()
182	        ?: runCatching { OffsetDateTime.parse(raw, DateTimeFormatter.RFC_1123_DATE_TIME).toInstant() }.getOrNull()
183	}
184
